questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: Regarding the administration and nature of the Nobel Prize, which of the following is correct?

options.text: The prizes are state-sponsored awards administered by the Swedish government.
The Nobel Peace Prize is the only category that can be awarded to an organization.
A candidate can nominate themselves if they provide sufficient evidence of their work.
The Nobel Prize in Economic Sciences was the first category established in 1901.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct: All Nobel prizes are for individuals except the Peace Prize, which can be given to institutions. Option A is incorrect: The prizes are independent of the state. Option C is incorrect: Self-nominations are not allowed. Option D is incorrect: The first prizes were in 1901, but Economic Sciences (Nobel Memorial Prize) was established later (Indian laureates in this field appear from 1998 onwards).
questions.takeaway: The Peace Prize is unique among Nobel categories for its eligibility criteria regarding institutions.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: All prizes can be conferred to individuals except for the Peace Prize which can be conferred upon an institution as well... Also, the Nobel Prizes are independent of the state.
